title: Does Rebuild use CGI
wikitext:
[]: Does Rebuild use CGI? Post by InstrumentalityOne » Wed Sep 29, 2010 2:12 pm I´ve always wondered if they used CGI in the movies. Some/A lot of parts definitely look drawn, but some things (Evas, Angels) look very artificial to me. Did they use CGI in 1.11/2.22? And if yes, how much did they use?

[]: Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Sep 29, 2010 2:24 pm Yes. They used a lot. Pop in your 1.11 DVD/BluRay, watch "Rebuild of Evangelion 1.01", it will show you how much CGI is in the movie.

[]: Post by facespace » Wed Sep 29, 2010 4:20 pm And in 2.22 they used even moar!

[]: Post by IrkenEvangelion » Wed Sep 29, 2010 4:22 pm They did use a lot.. but it's not that it looks bad, it's just noticeable.

[]: Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed Sep 29, 2010 4:27 pm Well, Shamshel from 1.11 looked pretty underwhelming in CGI. The rest of the CGI looked great, though.

[]: Post by NAveryW » Wed Sep 29, 2010 8:51 pm *Wheeze*

[]: Post by Nonoriri » Wed Sep 29, 2010 9:41 pm Buttloads.

[]: Post by Ornette » Wed Sep 29, 2010 9:50 pm OP Question answered. Discussion about Rebuild CG should go in the thread NAveryW linked: http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=6505

Article: Does Rebuild use CGI
[]: Does Rebuild use CGI? Post by InstrumentalityOne » Wed Sep 29, 2010 2:12 pm I´ve always wondered if they used CGI in the movies. Some/A lot of parts definitely look drawn, but some things (Evas, Angels) look very artificial to me. Did they use CGI in 1.11/2.22? And if yes, how much did they use?

[]: Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Sep 29, 2010 2:24 pm Yes. They used a lot. Pop in your 1.11 DVD/BluRay, watch "Rebuild of Evangelion 1.01", it will show you how much CGI is in the movie.

[]: Post by facespace » Wed Sep 29, 2010 4:20 pm And in 2.22 they used even moar!

[]: Post by IrkenEvangelion » Wed Sep 29, 2010 4:22 pm They did use a lot.. but it's not that it looks bad, it's just noticeable.

[]: Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed Sep 29, 2010 4:27 pm Well, Shamshel from 1.11 looked pretty underwhelming in CGI. The rest of the CGI looked great, though.

[]: Post by NAveryW » Wed Sep 29, 2010 8:51 pm *Wheeze*

[]: Post by Nonoriri » Wed Sep 29, 2010 9:41 pm Buttloads.
